extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions.

Key invariant: once opted_out becomes true it stays true (no transition ever resets it to false). The only transition that sets sent:=true is t2 (send_attempt when NOT opted_out), which by its own guard requires opted_out=false — so at the moment sent is set true, opted_out is false and the implication is vacuously satisfied.

opted_out is set true only by t1 (optout), and t1 simultaneously sets sent:=false. Therefore the state immediately after entering opted_out=true has sent=false. While opted_out remains true thereafter: t2 is permanently disabled (its guard fails), t3 (send_attempt while opted_out) sets sent:=false, t4 (tick) sets sent:=false, and a repeated t1 sets sent:=false. No enabled transition can set sent back to true while opted_out is true. An event with no matching enabled transition leaves state unchanged, so sent stays false.

Hence in every reachable state with opted_out=true, sent=false. The implication holds in all states (true when opted_out=false vacuously, true when opted_out=true since sent=false), so the global invariant holds for all executions, including those up to 7 events. No counterexample exists.
